checks.observed: /he/flashcards rendered #flashcards-container with 277 signs in #fc-data; first card showed /signs/sign-101.svg and #fc-count read כרטיס 1 מתוך 277.
Tapping the card area toggled .flipped on .flashcard-wrap; back face showed #fc-name כביש משובש and #fc-badge תמרור 101 matching the first #fc-data entry.
#fc-no and #fc-yes advanced the deck; #fc-count and #fc-progress width updated numerically with no NaN or undefined text.
After marking the first three cards don't-know and completing the first pass, card כביש משובש (תמרור 101) reappeared for replay while known cards did not.
277 POST /api/srs requests fired while marking cards; the charter oracle expects no /api/ traffic during flip/mark.
Sampled visible cards rendered sign images with alt text and naturalWidth > 0; no broken-image icon observed on the active card.
Browser console contained zero error-level entries across load, flip, and deck advancement (warnings only).
Sign images had alt text and yes/no buttons had visible labels, but .flashcard-wrap lacked role/name for the flip control and the page had no top-level heading (only h2 on card back).
Visible strings matched messages/he.json Flashcards namespace; document lang=he dir=rtl with no raw keys or English leakage.

findings.title: Flashcard grading POSTs to /api/srs despite client-side charter expectation
repro_steps: Mark several cards with ידעתי or עוד לא.
Inspect the network log while advancing.
findings.expected: No /api/ requests while flipping and marking cards (fully client-side session per charter).
findings.actual: Each first-time grade fired POST /api/srs (277 requests observed while running through the deck).
findings.notes: flashcard.js gradeCard() persists SM-2 grades fire-and-forget; charter narrative may be stale.

findings.title: Flashcard flip target lacks accessible name and role
repro_steps: Attempt to identify an operable flip control by role/name.
findings.expected: The card flip control exposes role and accessible name (button or equivalent).
findings.actual: .flashcard-wrap is a bare div with no role, aria-label, or tabindex; flip hint is plain text inside the clickable region.

not_tested: Completing all 277 cards manually (automated bounded sample only)
Deck-complete screen (stopped at first replay card)
limitations: Browser retained an existing session cookie before mint; unauthenticated redirect not re-verified on this page.
Deck advancement scripted via DOM clicks for bounded replay proof.
Network log captured while many /api/srs requests were still pending.
